title: Light of the World
subtitle: In the temple, the hidden God speaks the words for which they take up stones

stillness: Be still, and let the light come near.
We were born blind from the first Adam.
Let yourself be anointed now with the salve of faith.
Wait in the quiet, and listen.
gospel: Again Jesus spoke to them, saying:
I am the light of the world.
He who follows me does not walk in darkness,
but shall have the light of life.
When you have lifted up the Son of man,
then you shall know that I am he.
If you continue in my word,
you shall be truly my disciples,
and you shall know the truth,
and the truth shall make you free.
They answered: We are the seed of Abraham,
and we have never been slaves to anyone.
Amen, amen, I say to you:
everyone who commits sin is a slave of sin.
behold: He had just absolved the woman from her crime,
and now, lest any should doubt
whether the one they saw, true man, could forgive sins,
he deigns to show more openly the power of his divinity.
See the light of the world, going forth from the Father,
veiled in the cloud of flesh,
so tempered that men might bear it,
that through the man we might come to the Godhead.
Hear him provoke the Jews to the contest of his Passion:
when you have lifted me up upon the cross,
stretched out and raised high into the air,
then some of you shall know who I am —
I am he, God hidden beneath the flesh.
consider: He is the true light, who enlightens every man coming into this world.
Drawing near to him by faith and devotion, you are enlightened unto salvation;
drawing back from him, you are darkened unto hell.
The darkness to be feared is not of the eyes but of our ways;
and if of the eyes, not the outer but the inner,
where we tell apart not black and white, but the just and the unjust.
Let us do what he commanded, that we may not with shameless face long for what he promised.
Let him not say to us in his judgment:
Have you done what I commanded, that you should claim what I promised?
He says: I delay your knowing, that I may complete my Passion —
so that no one should despair, guilty in whatever crime,
when he sees murder forgiven to those who killed Christ.
Three ways we offend God and humble him:
by evil thoughts, by evil words, by evil works.
And there is a slavery harder than any slavery of men:
everyone who commits sin — noble or base, rich or poor, emperor or beggar — is a slave of sin.
O wretched slavery!
The slave of a man, worn out by his master's harsh commands, finds rest by fleeing;
but the slave of sin — where will he flee?
He drags himself with him wherever he flees.
An evil conscience does not flee from itself.
If you would know God, exalt him:
by contrition of heart, against evil thoughts;
by confession of mouth, against evil words;
by satisfaction of body, against evil deeds.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, who invite us to hear the word of God,
teach us to bear insults and injuries,
not to seek our own glory,
not to forsake the truth of life, of justice, and of doctrine to avoid scandal,
not to cease from preaching because of insult and injury repeated.
Jesus Christ, highest truth, goodness, justice, mercy,
you who are stoned and hidden from the lying and the malicious,
the unjust, the impious, the greedy, the unclean, the lustful, the proud, and the idle:
do not go out from the temple of my soul;
make me correctable, and in all things at one with you. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Lux mundi a Patre egrediens nube carnis tegitur; et sic contemperata toleranda hominibus efficitur, ut per hominem veniatur ad divinitatem.
latinJewel.english: The light of the world, going forth from the Father, is veiled in the cloud of flesh; and so tempered, it becomes bearable to men, that through the man we may come to the Godhead.
